messages.content:
You are a COBOL-to-English semantic synthesizer. Given an ordered list of Pass-2 propositions belonging to a single paragraph, produce a paragraph-level summary in the structured JSON format below. Respond with ONLY the JSON object — no prose, no code fence, no commentary.

Required response JSON schema:
{
  "paragraph": "<copy from request>",
  "group_label": "<short human-readable label, 2-5 words>",
  "semantic_pattern": "<one of: guard-with-override, accumulation, state-machine, delegation, sequential, conditional-branch, cics-interaction, file-io, unknown>",
  "summary": "<one-sentence paragraph-level description>",
  "member_seqs": [<int>, ...],
  "business_rules": [
    {"id": "BR-NNN", "rule": "<English statement>", "rule_type": "<guard|transform|lookup|audit|display>", "confidence": "<high|medium|low>"}
  ]
}

Rule 10: `semantic_pattern` MUST be one of the nine enum values. `unknown` is permitted ONLY when the paragraph is genuinely unclassifiable; it triggers a human-review gate and blocks merge. Do NOT use `unknown` as a default — prefer `sequential` for linear flows and `conditional-branch` for IF/EVALUATE gating.
Rule 9: all data names in business_rules[].rule text must be fully qualified if the underlying Pass-2 proposition carries a qualified name.

Program ID: CBCUS01C
Paragraph: CBCUS01C-MAIN
Proposition count: 10

Ordered propositions (seq → verb → proposition):
  seq=1  DISPLAY  →  Display output message
  seq=2  PERFORM  →  Execute the 0000-CUSTFILE-OPEN procedure
  seq=3  PERFORM  →  Execute a procedure
  seq=4  IF [IF END-OF-FILE = 'N']  →  When END-OF-FILE = 'N'
  seq=5  PERFORM [IF END-OF-FILE = 'N']  →  Execute the 1000-CUSTFILE-GET-NEXT procedure
  seq=6  IF [IF END-OF-FILE = 'N']  →  When END-OF-FILE = 'N'
  seq=7  DISPLAY [IF END-OF-FILE = 'N']  →  Display output message
  seq=8  PERFORM [IF END-OF-FILE = 'N']  →  Execute the 9000-CUSTFILE-CLOSE procedure
  seq=9  DISPLAY [IF END-OF-FILE = 'N']  →  Display output message
  seq=10  GOBACK [IF END-OF-FILE = 'N']  →  Return control to caller

Produce the paragraph-level semantic synthesis per the schema in the system prompt. Include every proposition's `seq` in `member_seqs`. Derive business rules ONLY from propositions that carry a real guard / transform / lookup / audit / display intent — do not invent rules for routine MOVE/SET/PERFORM statements.

Program ID: CBCUS01C
Paragraph: 1000-CUSTFILE-GET-NEXT
Proposition count: 16

Ordered propositions (seq → verb → proposition):
  seq=11  READ  →  Retrieve next record from file
  seq=12  IF [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00']  →  When CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00'
  seq=13  MOVE [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00'] [modifies=APPL-RESULT]  →  Set APPL-RESULT to 0
  seq=14  DISPLAY [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00']  →  Display output message
  seq=15  IF [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00']  →  When CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00'
  seq=16  MOVE [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00'] [modifies=APPL-RESULT]  →  Set APPL-RESULT to 16
  seq=17  MOVE [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00'] [modifies=APPL-RESULT]  →  Set APPL-RESULT to 12
  seq=18  IF [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00']  →  When CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00'
  seq=19  CONTINUE [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00']  →  Continue without action
  seq=20  IF [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00']  →  When CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00'
  seq=21  MOVE [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00'] [modifies=END-OF-FILE]  →  Set END-OF-FILE to 'Y'
  seq=22  DISPLAY [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00']  →  Display output message
  seq=23  MOVE [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00'] [modifies=IO-STATUS]  →  Set IO-STATUS to CUSTFILE-STATUS
  seq=24  PERFORM [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00']  →  Execute the Z-DISPLAY-IO-STATUS procedure
  seq=25  PERFORM [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00']  →  Execute the Z-ABEND-PROGRAM procedure
  seq=26  EXIT [IF CUSTFILE-STATUS = '00']  →  Exit the current paragraph

Produce the paragraph-level semantic synthesis per the schema in the system prompt. Include every proposition's `seq` in `member_seqs`. Derive business rules ONLY from propositions that carry a real guard / transform / lookup / audit / display intent — do not invent rules for routine MOVE/SET/PERFORM statements.
